channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US moves ahead on tariffs with investigations into computer chips and pharmaceuticals
item.description: The Trump administration has taken its next steps toward imposing more tariffs on key imports, launching investigations into imports of computer chips and products that contain them, chip making equipment and pharmaceuticals.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uber and Lyft drivers in California could get the right to unionize under this bill
item.description: Two California Democrats have introduced a bill that would allow rideshare drivers to bargain with gig companies, including Uber and Lyft, for better pay and certain benefits.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft releases out-of-band updates to fix reporting error
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency patches to fix an apparent reporting error in Active Directory (AD) Group Policy, which allows administrators to manage and configure user and computer settings in Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: America’s loss is China’s gain: Beijing is recruiting fired U.S. scientists
item.description: China appears to be pursuing a calculated effort to recruit recently laid-off U.S. scientists—particularly those with expertise in artificial intelligence—to relocate or contribute remotely to research operations based in Shenzhen.
item.dc:creator: Tim Bajarin, Fast Company

item.title: Judgment Day is coming… for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence has great potential in predicting outcomes. While AI can predict the likelihood of precipitation, it most likely won’t help you dress or prepare for inclement weather. That preparation involves more than the probability of rain or snow.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows Server 2025 domain controllers may lose connectivity after reboot, says Microsoft
item.description: Microsoft is warning IT administrators that their Windows Server 2025 domain controllers (DC) may lose network connectivity after they are restarted.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This wild phone battery rumor has me wondering whether iPhone will ever catch up to Android
item.description: The Xiaomi 16 has been rumored to come with a 7,000mAh battery, which would make it the first phone to cross the 7,000mAh mark. But it’s got me wondering whether Apple is going to pick up the pace with its own phone batteries any time soon.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: 4 time-saving Microsoft Teams tricks to make you more productive
item.description: For some, Microsoft Teams is a necessary evil: the modern day equivalent of an ever-ringing desk phone. But the fact is that in many organizations, it’s become an essential tool for communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Quantum computing could change science forever—if it works
item.description: Artificial intelligence has transformed how companies process data and make decisions—but Silicon Valley’s biggest players are already chasing what could be the next technological breakthrough: quantum computing.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: Intel to sell 51% Altera stake to Silver Lake amid cost cuts
item.description: Intel Corp. has agreed to sell a 51% stake in its programmable chips unit Altera to Silver Lake Management, making good on plans to start spinning off non-core assets.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta faces potential breakup with start of antitrust trial
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission is finally having its day in court against Meta Platforms Inc., arguing that the company must be broken up for illegally monopolizing the social media market after buying Instagram and WhatsApp more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia Announces $500 Billion AI Chip and Supercomputer Manufacturing Push in US
item.description: Nvidia to manufacture up to $500 billion of AI chips and supercomputers entirely in the U.S. over four years, amidst President Trump's trade war efforts.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: More than $35,000 recovered in stolen Bitcoin scam for Ohio woman
item.description: An Ohio woman who fell victim to a cryptocurrency scam has been refunded most of the nearly $42,000 in stolen Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: One day, you’ll fold your Mac and pop it in your pocket
item.description: iPads are about to become more productive, at least, that’s if the almost seer-like speculation is correct. Apple plans to give its “What’s a PC?” tablets even more Mac-like attributes this year, with details about its plans expected at WWDC 2025 in June.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sennheiser launches AirPods-like open earbuds, and if they're as good as the rest of its Accentum headphones, they'll be a bargain
item.description: The Accentum Open are lightweight, open earbuds with an attractively low price and a slim case design
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft warns that anyone who deleted mysterious folder that appeared after latest Windows 11 update must take action to put it back
item.description: If you deleted the folder in question, your PC is missing a security fix – here’s what you need to do to resolve the situation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Leaked Avengers: Doomsday images seemingly tease meeting between rival X-Men characters – and a Marvel hero who wasn't part of the original cast reveal
item.description: The first set leaks from Avengers 5 have been posted online and they reveal some interesting things about the MCU film.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The next big AI shift in media? Turning news into a two-way conversation
item.description: When people talk about how AI might reshape media, the term “hyper-personalization” comes up a lot. In broad terms, it means that AI can tailor the experience around your preferences—assuming it has enough data about you.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: For April, a large ‘dynamic’ Patch Tuesday release
item.description: This month’s Patch Tuesday release is large (126 patches), broad and unfortunately very dynamic, with several re-releases, missing files and broken patches affecting both the Windows and Office platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Social Security Administration ‘will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) unveiled Thursday that it would use the social platform X to make announcements going forward, instead of traditional press releases or memos typically posted to the agency's website.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Apple flew 600 tons of iPhones from India to the US to avoid Trump tariffs
item.description: Apple chartered six planes to fly 600 tons of iPhones into the US from India to avoid President Donald J. Trump’s tariffs, Reuters reports. That’s an estimated equivalent of up to 1.5 million smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk 'most unlikeable person' in US: House Democrat
item.description: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) called Elon Musk the “most unlikeable person” in the U.S. during a Thursday speech in support of his party’s push to oust the tech giant from his White House role by May 30.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Can't find your Android phone? Here's what to do to track it down
item.description: There are multiple ways to find your missing Android phone after it goes missing. One method involves using a smartwatch to ping your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is data fabric? How it offers a unified view of your data
item.description: What is data fabric? Data fabric is a type of architecture that aims to provide unified access to the data stored in various places across your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The problem with Google’s Pixel 9a
item.description: Let’s get one thing out of the way first: Google’s Pixel 9a is one heck of a device. I’ve had the latest Pixel gizmo in my man-claws for a couple o’ weeks now, thanks to a loaner review unit provided by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI
item.description: OMB’s new acquisition memo to buy AI details only four deadlines for agencies over the next year, but outlines a host of new concepts to follow. The post OMB expands on best ways agencies should buy AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
